user_query:
Please read input/specs.json and input/records.txt to complete a multi-part regex task:

1) Build and validate production-ready regex patterns that satisfy the specs for: emails (whitelist domains), https URLs, order IDs, public IPv4 addresses, and US phone numbers (for normalization). The input text contains [SKIP_START] ... [SKIP_END] sections that must be completely ignored for both extraction and normalization.

2) Extract all matches from input/records.txt that are outside the skip sections and save them to output/matches.json with this exact schema:
{
  "emails": [ {"match": string, "index": number, "namedGroups": {"user": string, "domain": string}} ],
  "urls": [ {"match": string, "index": number, "namedGroups": {"host": string, "path": string|null}} ],
  "order_ids": [ {"match": string, "index": number, "namedGroups": {"year": string, "month": string, "day": string, "code": string}} ],
  "ipv4_public": [ {"match": string, "index": number, "namedGroups": {"a": string, "b": string, "c": string, "d": string}} ]
}
Rules for matches.json:
- index is the 0-based character index of the match in the raw file (still ignoring skip sections for which matches must not exist at all).
- Each array must contain unique matches (no duplicates) and be sorted by index ascending.
- Named groups must be populated per the specs (use appropriate names).

3) Normalize all US phone numbers outside skip sections to canonical +1-AAA-BBB-CCCC using a regex find-and-replace with backreferences. Do not change any other text. Write the fully transformed text to output/normalized.txt.

4) Save all final patterns and flags you used to output/patterns_used.json in this exact shape:
{
  "email": {"pattern": string, "flags": string},
  "url": {"pattern": string, "flags": string},
  "order_id": {"pattern": string, "flags": string},
  "ipv4_public": {"pattern": string, "flags": string},
  "phone_normalize": {"pattern": string, "flags": string, "replacement": string}
}

5) Produce a token-by-token explanation of the final order ID regex you used (including anchors, groups, and any lookarounds) and save it to output/order_id_regex_explained.md. Your explanation must clearly mention the named groups year, month, day, and code.

Notes:
- Follow the domain whitelist and public IPv4 rules from input/specs.json.
- Treat [SKIP_START]...[SKIP_END] blocks as off-limits for both extraction and replacement.
- Ensure patterns avoid catastrophic backtracking and use appropriate flags for multiline text.
